name: Whole Wheat Sandwich Bread

ingredients.item: instant yeast 1 (0.25-oz.) package or 2 1/4 tsp. instant
flour made from whole wheat (390 g.)

method: To make tangzhong, stir together 12 cup water and 2
tablespoons flour in a small saucepan over medium-low heat
until a thicker slurry develops, about 2 to 3 minutes. Allow
cooling to room temperature after removing from the heat.
In a large mixing basin, combine yeast, milk, 1/4 cup water, and
honey; set aside for 15 minutes until frothy.
To the yeast mixture, add 2 34 cups whole wheat flour, cooled
tangzhong, oil, butter, and salt. During 2 minutes, or until the
dough is cohesive and sticky, mix for 1 to 2 minutes. Allow for
a 20-minute rest period after covering the bowl.
Gently take up one edge of the dough and pull softly to fold it
toward the other edge, using moist hands to prevent sticking and
handling the dough loosely to avoid ripping. Pour the mixture
over so that the insensitive is facing up; stretch and fold the
dough over the basin again. Allow 20 minutes for resting.
Continue the extending and folding technique two more times,
stopping between folds for 20 to 30 minutes.
Grease a 9" x 5" loaf pan lightly. Using oiled hands, pat dough
into a 9" × 11" rectangle on a lightly greased board. With the
dough, make a letter fold by folding one-third of the rectangle
over the center third, then folding the remaining third over the
middle third. Flatten the dough with your fingers, then roll it
into a log, starting on the short side. Place the log seam-side
down in the prepared pan, flatten it with even pressure to fill the
pan's bottom.
Lightly brush the top of the loaf with egg wash, then sprinkle
with various seeds or oats, if desired. To incorporate sources in
the dough, apply equal pressure. Allow 1 hour for proofing until
the middle of the loaf domes 12" over the top of the loaf pan.
Nearly an hour before you want to bake, preheat the oven to 375
degrees Fahrenheit.
Bake for 45 to 50 minutes, or until brown on top and internal
temperature reaches 200°.
